headline: The educators converge on a single thesis: agentic engineering value lives in the harness and the architecture, not the model.
body: Cole Medin's review of Google's agentic engineering whitepaper argues model selection is only 10% of AI coding success while context, tools, guardrails, and verification are the other 90%, placing context engineering and orchestration as the 2026 differentiators [0]. Cyril Imhof counters that agentic systems carry under-priced security risk, citing prompt injection, accidental data deletion, and unauthorized actions as justified grounds for business hesitation [1], and also runs an Anthropic-focused podcast segment [2]. The architecture thread continues in Angie Jones's case that coding agents push engineering work up to system-level design, decomposition, and state management [6], and in Nishant Gupta's production evaluation frameworks covering offline benchmark limits, continuous pipelines, drift, and hallucination detection for multi-step workflows [5]. Google Cloud anchors the infrastructure layer with Gemini 3's expanded reasoning and context window [12], plus ADK, Agent Registry, and Model Context Protocol integrations across BigQuery, Spanner hybrid search, and AI-native databases [4][8][9][10][11].
